dashboard.spec: Multi-project dashboard showing all marketing campaigns, per-project tabs, campaign status per layer
expectations: Dashboard loads with project tabs
Each project shows campaign count and layer progress
Projects are isolated — no data intermingling

layer-1-idea-definition.spec: Layer 1: Idea Definition — store one-sentence idea, audience, problem, payment condition, next step. Quality Gate 1 enforces all fields before outreach.
expectations: Can create new idea with all 5 required fields
Quality Gate 1 blocks launch if any field is empty or unclear
Ideas are scoped to their project

layer-2-audience-outreach.spec: Layer 2: Audience and Outreach — define audience (job role + company type), source leads, record fit reason, enroll in sending engine, run A/B messages, stop on reply. Quality Gate 2 enforces all rules.
expectations: Can define audience with inclusion/exclusion rules
Each lead has written fit reason
Two message versions run in parallel
Sending stops automatically on reply
Quality Gate 2 blocks outreach if rules not met

layer-3-conversation-qualification.spec: Layer 3: Conversation and Qualification — classify replies (not relevant, curious, interested, ready to evaluate), suggest next action, log exchanges, move leads through stages. Quality Gate 3 enforces accuracy.
expectations: Replies are classified into 4 categories
Next action requires <10 min from recipient
All exchanges logged
Quality Gate 3 blocks advancement if classification doesn't match

layer-4-proof-demonstration.spec: Layer 4: Proof and Demonstration — provide proof via summary, walkthrough, or trial. Landing page rules (problem, outcome, one CTA). Demo rules (<10 min, outcome before features). Quality Gate 4 enforces.
expectations: Can create proof in 3 forms (summary, walkthrough, trial)
Landing page shows problem clearly with single CTA
Quality Gate 4 blocks if proof shows features before outcomes

layer-5-paid-conversion.spec: Layer 5: Paid Conversion — propose paid step with scope, time period, price, success definition. Options: free trial, early access, pilot. Quality Gate 5 enforces concrete terms.
expectations: Can create offer with scope, duration, price, success criteria
Quality Gate 5 blocks if any field is missing or vague

layer-6-review-adjustment.spec: Layer 6: Weekly Review — messages sent, replies, stage advancement, biggest drop-off, single variable change. Only one major change per cycle. Quality Gate 6 enforces.
expectations: Weekly dashboard shows 5 review metrics
Only one variable can be changed per cycle
Quality Gate 6 blocks change without 30+ outreach attempts or documented reason

authentication.spec: Supabase Auth for multi-employee access. No special roles — internal product. Login/signup flow.
expectations: Login works with valid credentials
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login
No role-based restrictions beyond authentication

approval-modes.spec: Strict mode (all classifications require approval) and Relaxed mode (low-risk auto-advance). Toggle visible to founder.
expectations: Toggle between Strict and Relaxed mode
Default is Strict mode
In Relaxed mode, low-risk replies advance automatically
